emails.subject: LNG
emails.body: Scott Earnest announced in the staff meeting today that in a meeting he had earlier today with Jeff Shankman and Vince Kaminiski that Jeff announced that he had gotten the nod from Mark Frevert to manage the LNG book of business. (This sentence structure is bad, but you get the drift.) That is probably what Shankman has alluded to in terms of increased responsibility. I will assume that is in addition to gas, but I don't know that for sure. Scott has been working on setting up the risk books for LNG, working closely over the last several weeks with Vince Kaminski. We need to talk about this business and the best way to support it. Have you had any conversations with Jeff on this?

emails.subject: Re: LNG
emails.body: Re: LNG The only conversation I have had with Jeff regarding this topic was when I was in Houston and he had inquired as to when I was returning, stating that he was going to need some help in integrating another business within Gas Trading (and alluded to the possibility of it being Liquids related). My thoughts are that it would need to be supported out of my group since it is going to roll up to Jeff. Do you want me to set up a conference call with you, Scott Earnest and myself to discuss? Subsequent to that we should probably have a conference call with Jeff to get specifics. Let me know what I need to do on this. Brent Enron North America Corp. From: Sally Beck 06/05/2000 11:58 PM

emails.subject: Re: LNG
emails.body: I left a voice mail message for Dave Delainey this morning asking for clarification around the LNG business. As soon as I hear back from him I will let you know, and then based on that info we can decide on best next steps. Brent A Price 06/05/2000 09:18 PM
